system.name: Kinetic Gain Molecule Design Assistant
system.description: Research-use-only generative model for small-molecule scaffold ideation. Designed for academic medicinal chemistry teams and pre-IND discovery work. Does not process patient data. Not for clinical use; not a medical device.

clinical_context.indication: In silico generation and prioritization of small-molecule candidates targeting specific protein receptors. Used by computational chemists during the hit-to-lead phase of drug discovery.
clinical_context.intended_use: Research-use-only generative chemistry assistant. Output is a set of candidate molecular structures and prioritization scores for downstream wet-lab validation. The system is not authorized for any clinical or treatment-related use.

regulatory.notes: Research-use-only labeling enforced at deployment time; the system refuses prompts that imply patient-treatment context. IRB-equivalent review is required for any institution intending to use outputs in human-subjects research.

validation_studies.title: Benchmark of Kinetic Gain Molecule Design Assistant on the MOSES and GuacaMol generative-chemistry benchmarks
validation_studies.primary_outcome: Synthetic accessibility (SA score), drug-likeness (QED), novelty vs ChEMBL, diversity (internal-diversity) across 100k generated molecules
validation_studies.results_summary: SA score median 3.1 (vs 3.4 for ChemFormer baseline); QED median 0.62; novelty 0.81; internal-diversity 0.72. Top-1% candidates were synthesizable in-house in 6 of 10 spot-checks.

performance_metrics.measurement_population: Generated molecule pool across 100k samples on three target proteins (EGFR, BRAF, COX-2)
